Article Title: 'Remember me?' Parvesh Verma fires back after Kejriwal’s 'who are you' jab at Nitin Nabin
Article Text:
"National President is a very big deal, but do you even remember me or not?" Verma asked, taunting Kejriwal in return.

Poll Vote & Share your view Who do you believe holds more political credibility in this debate? Arvind Kejriwal Nitin Nabin Parvesh Verma Rekha Gupta 3k+ users shared opinion today 5k+ users already voted today 3k+ users shared opinion today Share Opinion

NEW DELHI: Delhi minister Parvesh Verma took a swipe at former chief minister Arvind Kejriwal after he taunted BJP national president Nitin Nabin over the Ram Mandir fund embezzlement row.As the political slugfest over the donation theft charges snowballed, back-and-forth remarks ensued between Kejriwal and Nabin.The BJP chief, while addressing a BJP event in Lucknow, had said, "Today, I want to tell Rahul Gandhi, Akhilesh Yadav, and Kejriwal that you do not consider the Hindu religion so weak that people will fall for your rhetoric."Rebutting Nabin's comments, Kejriwal asked, "Who are you?"Kejriwal's taunt prompted Parvesh Verma, who emerged as the BJP's giant slayer by defeating Kejriwal in the 2025 Assembly polls, to step into the verbal clash."National President is a very big deal, but do you even remember me or not?" Verma asked, taunting Kejriwal in return.Arvind Kejriwal, who made headlines when he led his Aam Aadmi Party to two historic victories in Delhi in 2015 and 2020, lost the New Delhi assembly seat to BJP's Parvesh Verma. The AAP chief lost his seat by over 4000 votes.Not just Kejriwal, all the big shots of the AAP except cheif minister Atishi lost their seat with the BJP storming back to power in Delhi after a gap of 27 years.Earlier, Rekha Gupta launched a blistering counterattack against Kejriwal over his "Who are you?" remark.The comment prompted a strong response from Delhi chief minister Rekha Gupta, who accused the former Delhi chief minister of allowing political frustration to turn into arrogance. In a post on X, Gupta first introduced Nabin while highlighting his political journey and credentials."Mr. Arvind Kejriwal, Mr. Nitin Nabin, aged 46, is the youngest-ever national president of the world's largest political party, the Bharatiya Janata Party. He has had the honor of representing the Bankipur Assembly constituency in Bihar for four consecutive terms. Starting as an activist in the Yuva Morcha, he has journeyed all the way to becoming the party's national president. Currently, he has been elected as a member of the upper house of Parliament in the world's largest democracy. This is a brief introduction to him," the chief minister said.She then launched a direct attack on Kejriwal, saying: "Everyone knows you're feeling desperate and disheartened. But it seems your ego is still riding high in the seventh heaven!" she said.Drawing a parallel with a similar remark once made by former West Bengal chief minister Mamata Banerjee about Union home minister Amit Shah, Gupta added, "By the way, Mamata ji once asked too, 'Who is this Amit Shah?' Time answers every question. Even Ravana's arrogance couldn't hold; who are you to think yours will?"Amid the heated political discourse, Shri Ram Janmabhoomi Teerth Kshetra Trust is set to hold a crucial meeting on Monday in Ayodhya amid the ongoing probe into the alleged embezzlement of donations.According to temple administration assistant Gopal Rao, the meeting will take place at 3 pm inside the Ram Temple premises. The Trust has asked all regular and ex-officio members to attend the deliberations."The trust meeting will take place at 3pm within the temple complex. Everyone has been notified; we expect everyone to attend," he said.The Trust is expected to discuss the resignations of general secretary Champat Rai and Anil Mishra, both of whom stepped down after being linked to the donation theft row.Champat Rai and Anil Mishra resigned from the Trust on June 26 after allegations of irregularities in donations surfaced, with questions being raised over their alleged role in the matter.
Article Summary: Parvesh Verma takes a swipe at former Delhi chief minister Arvind Kejriwal. Kejriwal taunted BJP national president Nitin Nabin over the Ram Mandir fund embezzlement row. Rekha Gupta launched a blistering counterattack against Kejriwal over his "Who are you?" remark.

Article Title: Cristiano Ronaldo sets unwanted World Cup record, becomes player with most...
Article Text:
At his last World Cup, Cristiano Ronaldo made headlines by attempting numerous shots yet shockingly, none translated into goal-scoring opportunities, establishing a record for missed chances. (AP Photo)

He took more shots than any player in recorded World Cup history without creating a single chance for a teammate. And yet, somehow, that stat tells you everything and nothing about Cristiano Ronaldo at the same time.As Ronaldo's final World Cup came to a tearful end on Monday night in Dallas, a remarkable and deeply uncomfortable statistic emerged from the data. Ronaldo attempted 17 shots at the 2026 FIFA World Cup without creating a single chance for a Portugal teammate across the entire tournament, the most by any player in a single World Cup edition since records began in 1966.The previous record had belonged to Mexico's Alberto Garcia Aspe, who registered 15 shots without a single chance created at France 1998. Poland's Jerzy Gorgon had 13 in 1974, South Africa's Katlego Mphela matched that tally in 2010, and Russia's Denis Cheryshev did the same in 2018. Ronaldo has now left them all behind by two shots, across a tournament where Portugal were ultimately knocked out by Spain in the Round of 16.The numbers cut both ways. On one hand, 17 shots in four matches shows a striker who never stopped trying, never stopped believing, never stopped demanding the ball and pulling the trigger.On the other, zero chances created in those same four matches with no key passes, no assists, no moments where Ronaldo's movement or touch set up a teammate, paints the picture of a forward who, at 41, was no longer the connective force he once was in Portugal's attack.Three goals in the tournament from those 17 shots is not a bad return. His conversion rate told one story. The chance creation number told another. And somewhere between those two statistics lives the complicated, fascinating, ultimately poignant truth of Ronaldo's 2026 World Cup.Coach Roberto Martinez continued to back him throughout, insisting Ronaldo's ability to attract defenders created space for others even when the data suggested otherwise. His teammates rallied around him publicly at every turn.But football's most unforgiving truth is that records do not care about loyalty or legacy.Seventeen shots. Zero chances created. Three goals. One final bow.
Article Summary: Cristiano Ronaldo attempted 17 shots at the 2026 FIFA World Cup without creating a single chance for a Portugal teammate. That is the most by any player in a single World Cup edition since records began in 1966. The previous record had belonged to Mexico's Alberto Garcia Aspe, who registered 15 shots without a single chances created at France 1998.

Article Title: 'This is our sport, not theirs': Jürgen Klopp blasts Trump and Infantino over controversial Folarin Balogun FIFA row
Article Text:
FILE - President Donald Trump holds up a red card during a meeting with FIFA president Gianni Infantino in the Oval Office of the White House, Tuesday, Aug. 28, 2018, in Washington. (AP Photo/Evan Vucci, File)





Trump's intervention sparks football's biggest controversy of the tournament

Referee Raphael Claus of Brazil shows a red card to United States' Folarin Balogun, right, during the World Cup round of 32 soccer match between the United States and Bosnia in Santa Clara, Calif., near San Francisco, Wednesday, July 1, 2026. (AP Photo/Julio Cortez)

Klopp: 'This is our sport, not theirs'

Calls for Infantino to resign gather momentum





Belgium end USA's World Cup despite Balogun reprieve

A fan reacts to a United States loss in a World Cup round of 16 soccer match against Belgium at the KC Live! entertainment district Monday, July 6, 2026, in Kansas City, Mo. (AP Photo/Charlie Riedel)

Jürgen Klopp has delivered one of the strongest criticisms yet of FIFA president Gianni Infantino and US President Donald Trump after the governing body controversially overturned Folarin Balogun's automatic World Cup suspension. The former Liverpool manager said political involvement in football disciplinary decisions "calls everything into question", as the fallout from FIFA's unprecedented intervention continued to spread across the sport.The controversy erupted after Trump publicly admitted lobbying Infantino to review Balogun's red card, before later claiming credit for the decision that allowed the United States striker to feature against Belgium in the FIFA World Cup Round of 16.Balogun was sent off during the United States' 2-0 Round of 32 victory over Bosnia and Herzegovina after receiving a straight red card for a challenge on Tarik Muharemović. Under FIFA's competition regulations, the dismissal automatically carried a one-match suspension, ruling him out of the last-16 meeting with Belgium.Days later, however, FIFA announced that the suspension would not be enforced immediately.Instead, citing Article 27 of the FIFA Disciplinary Code, the governing body suspended the ban for a one-year probationary period, making Balogun immediately eligible to return.The decision became even more controversial after Trump openly discussed his role in the process.Speaking at the White House, the US President said:"I'm the one that got them to do it."Trump explained that he had contacted FIFA president Gianni Infantino because he believed the dismissal was unjust."I saw the play, and I'm a person who loves sports. That wasn't a foul. That wasn't even an infraction. This referee, who is a little bit suspect if you check his past, made a call that nobody could believe. He's our best player, or one of our best players, and he gave him a red card."He also admitted his unfamiliarity with football's disciplinary system before deciding to intervene."I didn't know what the hell a red card was. When I found out, I said: 'You gotta be kidding!'"Explaining why he felt compelled to act, Trump added:"How would you feel if we took Lionel Messi, Cristiano Ronaldo or Harry Kane out? We have our best players, and they have to have their best, and if we win or we lose, it's fair."Earlier, Trump had also celebrated the outcome on Truth Social, writing:"Thank you to FIFA for doing what was right, and reversing a great injustice!"Although Infantino has repeatedly maintained FIFA's disciplinary panel acted independently, reports have claimed Trump discussed the matter with the FIFA president on three separate occasions before the suspension was overturned.Working as a television pundit during the World Cup before taking charge of Germany, Klopp became one of the highest-profile figures to publicly condemn the situation. According to The Telegraph, Klopp questioned whether political leaders should have any influence over football's disciplinary process."This is our sport, not theirs," Klopp said."If Donald Trump and Gianni Infantino really sorted this out between themselves, it is madness; it calls everything into question."His comments reflect growing concern across football that the integrity of FIFA's disciplinary system has been undermined by outside influence.The controversy has become the first major governance crisis of the tournament, with several football bodies expressing concern over the precedent created by FIFA's decision.The backlash has extended well beyond Klopp. According to reports, UEFA, the German Football Association and the Royal Belgian Football Association all criticised FIFA's handling of the case, while Belgium formally challenged Balogun's eligibility before the Round of 16 fixture, informing US Soccer that it contested the striker's participation and reserved "all further actions" available to it.Former Football Association chairman David Bernstein also criticised the decision, arguing that it undermined one of football's core principles."It hits at one of the beauties of football – the worldwide application across the world of regulations and rules."Former FIFA president Sepp Blatter was equally critical, warning against political interference in sporting decisions."Red cards are not overturned by political phone calls. They are overturned by rules, evidence, and independent bodies."He added:"If a US President intervenes with the FIFA President, and a player is suddenly cleared before a World Cup knockout match — the question is unavoidable: Quo vadis, FIFA?"Football must never become a playground for political power."Meanwhile, reports suggest England are now considering whether to challenge Jarell Quansah's suspension following his dismissal against Mexico, while France are exploring the possibility of overturning Michael Olise's booking against Paraguay—illustrating the wider consequences of FIFA's decision.Ultimately, Balogun's availability did little to change the outcome on the pitch.The Arsenal striker started against Belgium after his suspension was lifted, but the United States were comprehensively beaten 4-1 in Seattle. Charles De Ketelaere scored twice before Hans Vanaken capitalised on a defensive mistake from Matt Freese, with Romelu Lukaku adding a fourth in stoppage time to send Belgium into the quarter-finals.While the result prevented the controversy from escalating further through an American victory, the debate surrounding FIFA's decision continues to dominate discussion across the football world, with Klopp's intervention becoming the latest high-profile call for the sport's governing body to protect the independence of its disciplinary process from political influence.
Article Summary: Jürgen Klopp criticises FIFA president Gianni Infantino and US President Donald Trump. Klopp says political involvement in football disciplinary decisions "calls everything into question" FIFA controversially overturned Folarin Balogun's automatic World Cup suspension. Balogun was sent off during United States' 2-0 Round of 32 victory over Bosnia and Herzegovina.

Article Title: 'There's no point in continuing': Roberto Martinez quits as Portugal coach after World Cup exit
Article Text:
Roberto Martinez, right, gives instructions to Cristiano Ronaldo. (AP Photo)

Roberto Martinez has announced his departure as Portugal head coach following the team's elimination from the FIFA World Cup 2026, bringing an end to his three-and-a-half-year spell in charge.Portugal's campaign came to a close after a 1-0 defeat to Spain in the Round of 16 at Dallas Stadium. Mikel Merino scored the decisive goal in injury time to send Spain into the quarter-finals, while Portugal's hopes of lifting the World Cup were ended.Speaking after the match, Martinez confirmed that the clash against Spain was his final game as Portugal manager and said he saw no reason to continue after falling short of the team's ultimate objective."I came to Portugal to win the World Cup and I think that, without winning it, there's no point in continuing," Martinez told reporters. "The board and the president now have the opportunity to choose the new manager... my contract ends today. There isn't much more to say."The former Belgium coach, who took charge of Portugal in 2023, revealed that his departure had not been decided before the tournament. During his tenure, he guided the national side to the quarter-finals of UEFA EURO 2024 before leading them to the UEFA Nations League title in 2025.Confirming his exit, Martinez reflected warmly on his time with the national team."Yes, it's my last game for the national team," he said. "I'm very proud... I've felt welcomed as just another Portuguese person, in a very warm way. It's been a pleasure, a source of pride and a responsibility."The defeat also marked the end of Cristiano Ronaldo 's World Cup career. The 41-year-old had already confirmed before the match that the 2026 tournament would be his last appearance on football's biggest stage. He finishes his World Cup career with 11 goals in 27 matches and became the first player to score in six different editions of the tournament.Martinez paid an emotional tribute to his captain, praising Ronaldo 's professionalism and dedication throughout their time together."There aren't many Cristiano Ronaldos," Martinez said. "His dream was to win the World Cup, and he gave everything for that dream. He has been an example as a footballer, as a captain and as a human being."Reflecting on Portugal's defeat, Martinez felt his side had matched one of the tournament favourites for much of the contest but were ultimately undone by fine margins."We finished with sadness. It's not the result we wanted. The opponent is one of the favorites, but that didn't stop what we wanted to do. We were courageous defensively, aggressive, we defended very well. But what happens in the Round of 16 of a World Cup are important details."Martinez now leaves Portugal after overseeing a successful period that included a UEFA Nations League triumph, but with the World Cup title that both he and the Portuguese Football Federation had targeted remaining out of reach.
Article Summary: Roberto Martinez has announced his departure as Portugal head coach. Portugal lost 1-0 to Spain in the Round of 16 of the FIFA World Cup 2026. Martinez took charge of Portugal in 2023 and led them to the UEFA Nations League title.

Article Title: What really happened between Kylian Mbappe and Celeste Amarilla? Senator’s posts trigger major World Cup row
Article Text:
What really happened between Kylian Mbappe and Celeste Amarilla? Senator’s posts trigger major World Cup row (Image via Getty)

Who is Celeste Amarilla and why did she target Kylian Mbappe?

What happened between Kylian Mbappe and Orlando Gill after France vs Paraguay?

Kylian Mbappe’s response to Celeste Amarilla’s remarks

France moved into the 2026 FIFA World Cup quarterfinals after beating Paraguay 1-0 on penalties, but the biggest talking point came after the match ended. A heated exchange involving France captain Kylian Mbappe and Paraguay goalkeeper Orlando Gill turned into a much larger controversy. Soon after the game, Paraguayan senator Celeste Amarilla posted a series of racist comments aimed at Mbappe on X. The remarks sparked widespread criticism and drew a strong response from the French star, who accused Amarilla of spreading hatred and damaging Paraguay’s image during what had otherwise been a memorable World Cup campaign for the national team.Celeste Amarilla is a Paraguayan lawyer and politician who has served as a senator since 2023. She belongs to Paraguay’s Authentic Radical Liberal Party and is known for speaking openly on political issues.Her name entered the global football conversation after France’s World Cup win over Paraguay. Following the match, Amarilla used X to post several remarks directed at Mbappe. The comments were widely condemned because they included racist language and personal insults aimed at the France forward.Many people criticised the senator’s comments. What began as a football-related argument soon became a broader discussion about racism in sport and the responsibility of public officials when using social media platforms.The controversy started after the final whistle in Philadelphia. The Round of 16 match had already been tense, with several confrontations involving players from both teams during the game.One of the most talked-about moments happened after France secured victory. Paraguay goalkeeper Orlando Gill approached Mbappe and attempted to shake his hand. According to Gill, Mbappe did not respond to the gesture.Speaking after the match, Gill explained what happened from his point of view. “I tried to shake his hand, but since he didn’t pay me any attention, I lost my temper,” Gill said.Gill then threw the ball toward the France captain, creating another flashpoint after an already emotional contest. The incident drew significant attention online and in the media, but the focus soon shifted away from the players when Amarilla’s social media posts emerged.Mbappe did not stay silent after seeing Amarilla’s comments. The France captain responded publicly and strongly condemned the senator’s remarks. “Madame Celeste Amarilla, You are a despicable woman and unworthy of your position,” Mbappe wrote on social media.He continued by defending Paraguay’s players and supporters despite criticizing the senator. “You do not represent Paraguay, that country which has sweated passion and honor throughout the competition.” Mbappe also argued that Amarilla’s comments had overshadowed Paraguay’s achievements at the World Cup.“Through your recklessness and your brazen racism, the entire world has already forgotten the journey and the historic effort that your players accomplished during this World Cup, making way for an incompetent woman who gives the worst possible image of her country.”The France forward ended his message by making his position clear. “I will never allow people like her the freedom to spread their hatred and racism across the world.”While the controversy continues to generate discussion, France’s attention now turns to its quarterfinal clash against Morocco. Mbappe also remains level with Erling Haaland and Lionel Messi in the race for the tournament’s Golden Boot.
Article Summary: France beat Paraguay 1-0 on penalties to reach the 2026 World Cup quarterfinals. After the match, France captain Kylian Mbappe and Paraguay goalkeeper Orlando Gill had a heated exchange. Paraguayan senator Celeste Amarilla then posted a series of racist comments on X. The comments were widely condemned because they included racist language and personal insults aimed at the France forward. The France captain responded publicly and strongly condemned the senator's remarks.

Article Title: Lamine Yamal consoles tearful Cristiano Ronaldo after Portugal's World Cup heartbreak - Watch
Article Text:
Spain's Lamine Yamal, right, hugs Portugal's Cristiano Ronaldo (7) (AP Photo)

Spain's teenage star Lamine Yamal shared a touching moment with Cristiano Ronaldo after bringing an end to one of football's greatest World Cup careers on Monday.Moments after Spain sealed a dramatic 1-0 victory over Portugal in the Round of 16, the 18-year-old was among the first players to make his way to Ronaldo . As the Portuguese legend stood devastated following the final whistle, Yamal embraced the 41-year-old in a gesture of respect after his sixth and final FIFA World Cup came to an emotional close.The contest in Dallas had been billed as a meeting between two generations. On one side was Yamal, the teenage sensation leading Spain's new era. On the other stood Ronaldo, one of the greatest players in football history, making his final appearance on the World Cup stage.In the end, it was youth that prevailed.Spain booked their place in the quarter-finals thanks to Mikel Merino's dramatic winner in the first minute of second-half stoppage time. After initially winning a free-kick, Merino quickly restarted play, continued his run into the box and finished from Ferran Torres' pass to beat Diogo Costa and send Spain through.The victory also ended Ronaldo's pursuit of the only major trophy missing from his glittering career.The Portuguese captain, who remains the all-time leader in men's international football with 146 goals and 233 appearances, walked off the pitch in tears after the final whistle.His dream of guiding Portugal to a first World Cup title had once again slipped away.Ronaldo did have opportunities during the match but found Spain goalkeeper Unai Simón in inspired form. His best chance arrived in the 37th minute when João Félix's header deflected into his path. Ronaldo improvised with an acrobatic backheel effort, but Simón recovered brilliantly to make the save.Portugal threw everything forward during a lengthy period of stoppage time, with Bernardo Silva going close to finding an equaliser, but Spain's defence held firm to record a sixth consecutive World Cup clean sheet.For Ronaldo, the defeat marked the end of a remarkable World Cup journey that began in 2006, when Portugal reached the semi-finals. He later experienced a Round of 16 exit in 2010, a group-stage elimination in 2014, another Round of 16 defeat in 2018 and a quarter-final finish in 2022 before his final campaign ended at the same stage against Spain.
Article Summary: Spain beat Portugal 1-0 in the Round of 16 to reach the World Cup quarter- finals. Mikel Merino scored the winner in the first minute of second-half stoppage time. Portugal captain Cristiano Ronaldo walked off the pitch in tears after the final whistle.

Article Title: After all the drama... Balogun's controversial FIFA reprieve ends in World Cup heartbreak with little impact
Article Text:
Folarin Balogun chases the ball during the World Cup round of 16 match against Belgium. (AP Photo)

One free-kick earns his only major contribution



ALSO READ:

From FIFA storm to quiet exit



After days of intense debate, political intervention and unprecedented controversy over his red-card suspension being overturned, Folarin Balogun's return to the United States lineup ended with little to show as Belgium stormed to a convincing 4-1 victory in the FIFA World Cup Round of 16 on Monday.Balogun, whose availability dominated global football headlines after FIFA suspended his automatic one-match ban, struggled to influence proceedings as the Americans crashed out of the tournament. The Monaco forward finished without a goal and managed only 10 touches in the first half — the fewest of any player on the pitch at that stage — in a performance that contrasted sharply with the attention surrounding his reinstatement.The defeat also extended a painful trend for the United States, who have now been eliminated in the Round of 16 in six of their seven appearances at that stage of the FIFA World Cup , including each of their last four knockout appearances.Balogun's most meaningful involvement came in the 31st minute when he was brought down around 25 yards from goal by Belgian defender Brandon Mechele. Malik Tillman curled the resulting free-kick into the net to briefly level the scores at 1-1.Moments before the set-piece, Balogun attempted to lift the home crowd by waving his arms toward the stands, but Belgium quickly regained control and restored their advantage just over a minute later.The United States repeatedly looked to exploit Balogun's pace behind Belgium's defence, but the striker found goalkeeper Thibaut Courtois in inspired form. His best opportunity arrived in the 82nd minute, only for Courtois to deny his left-footed effort. Balogun was eventually substituted for Haji Wright in second-half stoppage time.Balogun's participation had overshadowed the build-up after FIFA took the extraordinary step of suspending his automatic one-match ban following his dismissal against Bosnia-Herzegovina.The striker had received a red card after Brazilian referee Raphael Claus reviewed an incident in which Balogun stepped on an opponent's ankle, automatically ruling him out of the Belgium clash.However, following a phone call between US President Donald Trump and FIFA president Gianni Infantino, FIFA's disciplinary committee invoked Article 27 of its disciplinary code to suspend the sanction for a one-year probationary period. Balogun remained eligible to play but was fined $40,000, a penalty that could be paid by the US Soccer Federation.The decision sparked widespread criticism across football. UEFA accused FIFA of having "crossed a red line", Belgium challenged Balogun's eligibility, and supporters chanted "FIFA Mafia" during their march to Seattle's Lumen Field before kickoff.Balogun arrived in the knockout stage having enjoyed a memorable tournament, scoring three goals to equal Landon Donovan's tally from the 2010 World Cup — the joint second-most by an American at a single edition behind Bert Patenaude's four in 1930. He had also become the first U.S. player since Patenaude to score twice in a World Cup match.But after days of unprecedented controversy and worldwide scrutiny, Balogun's much-discussed return ultimately proved to be a blink-and-you'll-miss-it cameo, as Belgium comfortably ended the United States' hopes of a home World Cup fairy tale.
Article Summary: Balogun struggled to influence proceedings as the Americans crashed out of the tournament. Monaco forward finished without a goal and managed only 10 touches in the first half. The defeat extended a painful trend for the United States, who have now been eliminated in the Round of 16 in six of their seven appearances at that stage of the FIFA World Cup.

Article Title: The Penguins’ multi-million Egor Chinakhov gamble exposes the NHL’s new, expensive reality
Article Text:
Egor Chinakhov’s new Penguins deal puts Pittsburgh’s bet on upside against an NHL market that keeps getting more expensive. (Image via Getty)

Egor Chinakhov’s Penguins breakout made the bet possible, not safe

The NHL’s old price list is gone, and Kyle Dubas knows it

“Pavel Mintyukov isn't ~$7.4 million-caliber yet and doesn't project to be worth that over the next five years, either. The offer sheet effect is real and shaking up the Ducks' cap picture.”

The Pittsburgh Penguins paid Egor Chinakhov $18.75 million for three years. The number looks as loud as it gets because it is.The deal, announced Sunday, July 5, is not really about one winger’s breakout stretch. It is about what the NHL is becoming. The Hockey News’ Kelsey Surmacz reported that Chinakhov’s extension came during a larger restricted free-agent signing day for Pittsburgh. Arturs Silovs, Daniel Gustafsson, and Joel Blomqvist also signed, but Chinakhov’s $6.25 million average annual value is the one that tells the story.Chinakhov gave the Penguins a real reason to gamble. After Pittsburgh acquired him from the Columbus Blue Jackets in a late-December change-of-scenery trade, he scored 18 goals and added 18 assists in 43 games.Across the full 2025-26 season, Chinakhov finished with 21 goals and 42 points in 72 games. Both were career highs. Mike J. Asti of Yardbarker noted that his Pittsburgh-only production would have set career highs on its own.Chinakhov had not been this player before. His previous best season came in 2023-24, when he had 16 goals and 29 points in 53 games. His young career before Pittsburgh had been uneven and affected by injuries.So, yes, there is risk here. Chinakhov is 25. He has one strong Penguins stretch, not years of proof. His salary now sits above Bryan Rust and Rickard Rakell, two more established Pittsburgh wingers.That is the part critics will grab first. It is also the easiest part of the deal to understand.Kyle Dubas is paying before the obvious breakout gets priced like a luxury item. Chinakhov showed a high-end shot, better playmaking, and top-six chemistry in Pittsburgh. Surmacz also noted that much of his production came at even strength because he was not getting top power-play minutes.If Chinakhov produces like this with more runway, $6.25 million stops looking strange quickly.The uncomfortable part is that Pittsburgh’s gamble does not look wild in the current market. It looks early.The NHL has spent the past few weeks reminding teams that waiting can be expensive. Surmacz pointed to Brady Tkachuk being traded to the Florida Panthers for three first-round picks and a second-round pick. Bowen Byram returned the 2026 No. 4 pick to the Buffalo Sabres, then signed with the Chicago Blackhawks for six years at a $12.5 million average annual value.The Seattle Kraken reportedly offered three first-round picks and a rostered NHL player for Dallas Stars forward Jason Robertson before Robertson declined to extend there. Then the Philadelphia Flyers sent Anaheim Ducks center Leo Carlsson an $18 million offer sheet that carried four first-round picks in compensation.Anaheim then signed Pavel Mintyukov to a five-year deal worth $7.2 million annually. Hockey analyst Shayna Goldman wrote on X,That is the market Chinakhov walked into. Young upside now costs real money before it becomes certainty. Bridge deals no longer feel automatic. Offer sheets have teeth. First-round picks are flying around in conversations for high-end talent.Pittsburgh’s deal still has a clear failure point. If Chinakhov settles as a useful winger instead of a top-six scorer, the Penguins overpaid.The better-case scenario is why Dubas made the move. Chinakhov does not need to become a superstar for the contract to work. He needs to make last season look like a start, not a spike. If that happens, the Penguins did not lose their minds. They read the market before it got worse.
Article Summary: The Pittsburgh Penguins paid Egor Chinakhov $18.75 million for three years. The deal, announced Sunday, July 5, is not really about one winger’s breakout stretch. It is about what the NHL is becoming. The Penguins bet on upside against an NHL market that keeps getting more expensive.

Article Title: Decoding the selection call that ended Sanju Samson's T20 World Cup high - and the road ahead
Article Text:
India's Sanju Samson (ANI Photo)

Will Gambhir and Co. travel to Zimbabwe?

Changes in the Asian Games squad

Poll Vote & Share your view Do you agree with the decision to exclude Sanju Samson from the squad for Zimbabwe? Yes, it was the right decision No, he deserves another chance 3k+ users shared opinion today 5k+ users already voted today 3k+ users shared opinion today Share Opinion

India squad for the 2026 Asian Games:

First, Captain Suryakumar Yadav , and now Player of the Tournament Sanju Samson . Almost four months after winning the T20 World Cup, India have decided to move on from two key figures of the squad that defended the title at home. While Ajit Agarkar , chairman of the men's senior selection committee, held a press conference explaining the move regarding SKY, much was left to the imagination and interpretation when Samson was excluded from the Shreyas Iyer-led squad for Zimbabwe.Not long ago, Samson was the toast of the nation for his knocks in the business end of the multi-nation tournament but the selectors have been wary of the inconsistency on either side of those outings. The opener only made the World Cup XI because the left-handed top-order struggled against off-spin. He repaid the faith, but the frustrating pattern of inconsistency returned even in the IPL and subsequent international fixtures. Add to this a red-hot Vaibhav Sooryavanshi making life uncomfortable for the decision-makers.Samson was the coaching set-up's original choice and received backing in the press conferences, but the other crucial stakeholders in Indian cricket were not willing to make Sooryavanshi wait in the wings. The argument was simple: "How can you make him carry drinks? The whole argument of 'he will learn' sounds so kiddish right now.You don't make him learn by carrying towels and drinks. You throw him in the deep end to swim when he is riding high on confidence," explains a well-placed source.Someone had to make way, and the options were Abhishek Sharma, Ishan Kishan , and Samson. While the southpaws have managed to stay amongst the runs, consistency has never been Samson's friend. Whether this sends the right message to the team and players is a separate discussion, but this move was purely aimed at giving Sooryavanshi the rope he has earned, even if it comes at the cost of snapping it for someone else.Prabhsimran Singh's maiden international call-up sends another message. Even if Kishan, Sharma, or Sooryavanshi have failures or inconsistent outings, Prabhsimran is ready to grab his chance, and it could well be a tough road back for the experienced Samson. While he hasn't been the only player missing from the squad playing in England, he is the only one-format player left out. Harshit Rana, Arshdeep Singh, Washington Sundar and Axar Patel have also missed the cut. Initially, there were suggestions to send a second-string side to Zimbabwe, under a new captain, but this did not translate into anything meaningful.There are murmurs that the regular support staff might skip the Zimbabwe tour. While TimesofIndia.com couldn't confirm, discussions have occurred because the turnaround time between the ODIs against England and the Zimbabwe series is very less. It remains to be seen whether the Gambhir-led support staff boards the flight or if the Indian cricket board sends coaches from the CoE panel.India announced their squad for the Asian Games last month, but TimesofIndia.com can confirm that there could be some last-minute changes. The Board of Control for Cricket in India (BCCI) is likely to approach the relevant authorities with the request, if it has not already done so. The rules permit them to do so, and exceptions can be made to even accommodate a player not part of the initial long list.Shreyas Iyer (C), Abhishek Sharma, Sanju Samson (WK), Ishan Kishan (WK), Shivam Dube, Tilak Varma (VC), Nitish Kumar Reddy, Axar Patel, Washington Sundar, Varun Chakaravarthy, Ravi Bishnoi, Jasprit Bumrah, Harshit Rana, Arshdeep Singh, Vaibhav Sooryavanshi
Article Summary: Sanju Samson has been left out of India's ODI squad for Zimbabwe. Samson was the toast of the nation for his knocks in the T20 World Cup. Harshit Rana, Washington Sundar and Arshdeep Singh have also missed the cut.

Article Title: F1 visa rejected on 214(b)? Former visa officer explains what to do now, before applying for it again
Article Text:
Former visa officer explains what to do after your F1 visa gets denied.

What do visa officers write during a refusal?

Ties to the country are different for B1/B2 and F1

What to do after an F1 visa refusal?

Former visa officer Yvette Bansal explained that most of the F1 visa applicants in India are getting rejected on 214(b), and it means teh visa has been refused on either of the following grounds: whether you will use the visa class appropriately, whether you are able to finance your education, if you are a genuine student or not. "It's not a permanent ban. It is a refusal that another visa officer can choose to overturn, but the refusal will always be on your record," Bansal said in a podcast.Bansal, who worked as a visa officer in the Mumbai consulate, said visa officers write notes when they refuse a case, and that note also stays in the record. Depending on how harsh the note is the applicant may get another shot at an F-1 visa. "It's like a softer 'I am not sure about this person' versus harsher 'this person is straight up lying to me'. These refusal notes do go a long way," Bansal said adding that in some cases these refusals are hard to overcome."If you feel like you have a strong case but the officer did not give you a fair chance or you really got nervous at the window, you can try again. But if the officer spends 8-9 minutes with you going over your finances, and you gave your best shot, then the refusal might be a little harsher, and so you should strengthen your case before applying again," Bansal said.Bansal explained that 'ties to the country' are different for tourist visa applicants and student visa applicants. For B1/B2 visas, officers check if you have property here, but for F1, officers check if you have a lot of education here, which you won't risk in the US by becoming an illegal. If a student has a family business, it is also a tie -- the more you have to lose if you become an illegal in the US are the more ties you have to your home country.The consular officer does not have access to your grades, so a better university will show your aptitude, Bansal said, adding that only a better university, however, can't clear the interview. The entire story has to be cohesive -- your current degree, your future degree and what you plan to do. "In India, you have to wait for a year to get another shot at an F-1 visa. So applicants who got refused should upskill themselves, look for an internship, and seek a bump in pay if they are working," Bansal said.
Article Summary: Most F1 visa applicants in India are getting rejected on 214(b), a former visa officer said. Officers write notes when they refuse a case, and that note also stays in the record. In India, you have to wait for a year to get another shot at an F-1 visa.

Article Title: 'Gautam Gambhir knows this hasn't been fair to Sanju': Ashwin questions Samson's omission
Article Text:
India's batsman Sanju Samson (PTI Photo)

Ash Ki Baat

Poll Vote & Share your view Should Sanju Samson have been given more opportunities despite his recent form? Yes, he deserves more chances No, selection should be based on performance 3k+ users shared opinion today 5k+ users already voted today 3k+ users shared opinion today Share Opinion

Former India off-spinner Ravichandran Ashwin has expressed concern over Sanju Samson 's sudden fall in India's T20I pecking order, questioning whether outside pressure and social media narratives are beginning to influence selection decisions.Samson, who was named Player of the Tournament during India's successful T20 World Cup campaign earlier this year, has endured a dramatic change in fortunes. After registering three successive single-digit scores against Ireland and England, he was dropped from the playing XI in Manchester, with teenage opener Vaibhav Sooryavanshi handed his much-awaited international debut. India lost the match by four wickets, while Sooryavanshi managed 14 runs.The wicketkeeper-batter's struggles continued when he was left out of India's squad for the upcoming three-match T20I series against Zimbabwe, with Prabhsimran Singh earning the call-up instead after an impressive IPL 2026 campaign for Punjab Kings.Speaking on his YouTube channel, Ashwin admitted he was surprised by how quickly Samson had been sidelined despite his recent contributions."Right now, I’m concerned. I’m worried because Sanju Samson has been dropped from the team so quickly.What will Sanju be thinking now? How motivated will he be when he goes to practice in the nets?" Ashwin said.The former spinner also suggested India could look at reshuffling the batting order rather than pushing Samson out altogether. With Abhishek Sharma, Vaibhav Sooryavanshi and Ishan Kishan all being left-handers, Ashwin felt Samson could slot in at No. 3."At the moment, you already have three left-handers in the top order: Abhishek Sharma, Vaibhav Suryavanshi and Ishan Kishan. Now, they will consider Sanju for No. 3, I’m telling you. Everyone knows, including the coaching staff and Gautam Gambhir, that this hasn’t been fair to Sanju. So now they’ll have to manage the situation somehow."Ashwin acknowledged that tough calls are part of international cricket but argued that players who have consistently repaid the team's faith deserve a longer run before being discarded. In his view, Samson had earned that backing through his performances.The veteran also raised a broader concern over whether external opinion was beginning to shape decisions within the Indian setup. Sooryavanshi's selection had been widely demanded by fans following his record-breaking IPL season, and Ashwin questioned whether that public sentiment had found its way into the selection process."I’m concerned about one thing. Are social media narratives and all the outside noise finding their way into the team? Is that influencing the decision-making? I’ve been watching cricket for a long time and have seen many leaders in the Indian team. They never allowed outside noise to enter the dressing room, and that’s exactly how it should be," he said.India will travel to Zimbabwe later this month for a three-match T20I series in Harare on July 23, 25 and 27. Samson's absence from the squad marks another setback for the 31-year-old, whose place has gone to Prabhsimran Singh after the latter's strong domestic and IPL performances.
Article Summary: Ravichandran Ashwin expresses concern over Sanju Samson's sudden fall in India's T20I pecking order. Ashwin questions whether outside pressure and social media narratives are beginning to influence selection decisions. Former spinner suggests India could look at reshuffling the batting order rather than pushing Samson out altogether.

Article Title: 'Stuffed in sack, dumped in pond alive': Autopsy reveals how Baruipur girl was gang-raped, brutalised to death; 3 held
Article Text:
Police have charged three suspects with gang-rape, murder, and destruction of evidence. One suspect was lynched by a mob while another escaped custody.

Want death for culprits: Girl’s mom

Poll Vote & Share your view Do you think enough is being done to ensure justice for victims of sexual violence? Yes, the system is effective No, more needs to be done 3k+ users shared opinion today 5k+ users already voted today 3k+ users shared opinion today Share Opinion





KOLKATA: The 12-year-old schoolgirl whose body was recovered from a local pond in Baruipur on Sunday was sexually abused, brutally tortured, packed into a sack and hurled into the water body when she was still alive, the autopsy report has indicated.The report says her private parts bore marks of horrific injuries, including burns, and the back of her head had a blunt injury caused either by a blow or by forcefully hitting it against a hard surface, leading to significant bleeding. The lungs contained muddy water, suggesting she was still breathing when dumped in the pond. The report lists excessive blood loss and suffocation from drowning as the likely causes of death. The time of death is believed to be Saturday night.The autopsy report prompted police to slap BNS sections for gang-rape, murder, destruction of evidence, criminal conspiracy and Pocso sections against the three prime suspects - Ananda Sardar, Dibakar Sardar and Prabhas Mondal. All three were arrested on Monday. A fourth suspect, Indrajit Tanti, was lynched by a mob on Sunday. A six-member Special Investigation Team has detained three others, one of whom has political connections.Locals and the victim's kin claimed they had caught Ananda and handed him over to cops on Sunday but he mysteriously went missing from the Suryapur police camp.Prabhas told cops Ananda had hatched the plot to abduct the girl and demand Rs 50,000 as ransom from her father, and he had handed over the girl to Dibakar. "Prabhas claimed Ananda tortured and strangled her but he was not present at the time," an officer said. Cops, however, suspect this is not the entire truth.Prabhas was seen walking with the girl in a CCTV footage at 4.10 pm on Saturday. He was the one who took locals and cops to the pond where the body was found, about a km from the victim’s home on Sunday morning.Police on Monday produced Prabhas and Dibakar in Baruipur court, where no lawyer came forward to defend the duo. Both were sent to police custody for 14 days.The victim’s mother said she wants police to ensure the culprits are punished by death. “We want these devils to suffer the same way as my child suffered before dying. And we want them punished as soon as possible. Many such incidents have happened before. We never saw justice being served. Now a new govt is in office. We hope to get justice,” she said.In view of the lynching and arson in Baruipur on Sunday, cops on Monday clamped section 163 of BNSS (previously Sec 144) across Baruipur, Sonarpur, and Narendrapur police stations of Baruipur subdivision.Even as the probe into the rape-murder was on, another body was fished out from the same pond where the minor’s body was found. It was identified as that of Krishnakanta Haldar, 30, who was missing for the past three days. Police, however, ruled out any connection of this death with the rape-murder.
Article Summary: Police have charged three suspects with gang-rape, murder, and destruction of evidence. A fourth suspect, Indrajit Tanti, was lynched by a mob on Sunday. A six-member Special Investigation Team has detained three others, one of whom has political connections. The victim's mother said she wants police to ensure the culprits are punished by death.

Article Title: After $130M Lakers payday, Walker Kessler shares major life update with Miss America fiancée
Article Text:
Walker Kessler got engaged to Abbie Stockard. Image via: kidkessler/ Instagram

New Lakers star Walker Kessler gets engaged to Miss America



How Walker Kessler and Abbie Stockard's love story started at Auburn



What Walker Kessler's engagement and contract mean for the Lakers



Walker Kessler got engaged to Abbie Stockard, Miss America 2025, over the Fourth of July weekend, days after signing a four-year, $130 million deal with the Los Angeles Lakers in a sign-and-trade with the Utah Jazz.The couple met at Auburn University, where Kessler's mother first spotted Stockard performing as a dancer at a basketball game. Kessler, one of the NBA's top shot blockers, is expected to anchor the Lakers' frontcourt alongside Luka Doncic and Austin Reaves following LeBron James' departure, though a shoulder injury limited him to five games last season.Walker Kessler capped a whirlwind week by getting engaged to Abbie Stockard, Miss America 2025, over the Fourth of July weekend, just four days after signing a four-year, $130 million deal with the Los Angeles Lakers. Stockard shared the news on Instagram alongside a photo of her ring, writing that their story was "one that only the Lord could have written."The engagement caps an 18-month stretch that took the couple from Kessler watching Stockard win her pageant crown courtside in Utah to planning a wedding in Los Angeles. Kessler, acquired via sign-and-trade from the Utah Jazz, is expected to anchor a retooled Lakers frontcourt alongside Luka Doncic and Austin Reaves as the franchise moves on from LeBron James this offseason.Kessler and Stockard met while both attended Auburn University, though the credit for the introduction belongs to Kessler's mother. During his rookie season, she spotted Stockard performing as a dancer at an Alabama-Auburn game and snapped a photo to send her son. Kessler brushed it off at first, but weeks later his mother tracked down Stockard's name through Auburn coach Bruce Pearl and passed it along. Kessler messaged her on Instagram, and the relationship took off from there.Stockard, a nursing student pursuing pediatric medicine, won Miss Alabama in 2024 before capturing the Miss America title in January 2025, becoming the fourth Alabama native to hold the crown. Kessler watched her win from a dinner with Jazz head coach Will Hardy, a moment that went viral for his stunned reaction. Reflecting on the milestone this week, Kessler admitted, "Dating Miss America was not on my bingo card. "Kessler's shoulder injury limited him to just five games last season, but the Lakers still viewed him as the rim-protecting, lob-catching center Doncic had been requesting since his arrival in Los Angeles. The sign-and-trade deal sent two unprotected first-round picks and two future swaps to Utah, a steep price that reflects how thin the market for available centers was this summer. Kessler finished third in Rookie of the Year voting behind Orlando's Paolo Banchero and has steadily built a reputation as one of the league's premier shot blockers since entering the NBA in 2022.With James gone and the Lakers fully committed to building around Doncic, Kessler now carries the dual weight of a $130 million contract and a starting job on a team expected to contend immediately. He arrives in Los Angeles with a fiancée, a fresh payday, and a health question mark still to answer on the court, a combination that will define how quickly the Lakers' new-look frontcourt comes together this season.
Article Summary: Walker Kessler signed a four-year, $130 million deal with the Los Angeles Lakers. He got engaged to Abbie Stockard, Miss America 2025, over the Fourth of July weekend. The couple met at Auburn University, where Kessler's mother first spotted Stockard.

Article Title: Luka Doncic leaves LeBron James behind, makes surprising Nikola Jokić pick in rapid-fire round
Article Text:
Luka Doncic and LeBron James. Image via: Adam Pantozzi/ NBAE via Getty Images

Luka Doncic leaves LeBron James behind, makes surprising Nikola Jokić pick in rapid-fire round



Luka Doncic of the Lakers (Image via Getty)

Fans react to Luka Doncic's Nikola Jokić pick over LeBron James

Luka Doncic picked Nikola Jokić over LeBron James and several other NBA legends during a rapid-fire "ultimate teammate" interview posted July 6 by Instagram user Klemen Kopina. The pick drew attention because James recently informed the Lakers he will sign elsewhere in free agency, ending his run with the franchise.Reports describe Doncic and James as close with no tension between them, and Doncic's answer is being read as a reflection of a hypothetical 2-on-2 fit rather than any real ranking. Fans reacted with a mix of amusement and speculation about Doncic's future preferences at center.Luka Doncic picked Nikola Jokić over LeBron James without hesitation during a rapid-fire interview posted July 6, a detail that stands out given James is no longer his Los Angeles Lakers teammate. Instagram user Klemen Kopina asked Doncic to choose an "ultimate" teammate for a hypothetical 2-on-2 game across a list of NBA legends, and Jokić kept winning.Doncic first chose Jokić over Oklahoma City's Shai Gilgeous-Alexander, then kept the streak going against Shaquille O'Neal, Magic Johnson and Victor Wembanyama. When James entered the bracket, Doncic picked Jokić again without pause. The run ended only when Larry Bird came up, and the interview closed with Doncic taking Michael Jordan over Bird.James informed the Lakers earlier this offseason that he intends to sign elsewhere for the 2026-27 season, ending an eight-year run in Los Angeles that included a 2020 championship. Reporting around the split has consistently described James and Doncic as close, with no indication of any rift, so the exercise reads less like a shot at James and more like Doncic weighing teammate fit in a 2-on-2 setting, which rewards a very different skill set than a full 5-on-5 lineup.The clip spread quickly, and reactions split along a few lines. One fan, @JBone9641, pushed back on any suggestion of tension, writing, "there's been 0 reports of tension." Another, @CoolHandMe33, framed it as a shift in perspective, posting, "He wouldn't have said Jokic over LeBron a few months ago." A third, @vegasmirabet, put it simply: "Jokic over LeBron is spicy, but Luka knows that game better than anyone."Whether the moment carries any real weight beyond a fun interview answer remains to be seen, but it lands at a pointed moment for the Lakers. With James gone and Doncic now the unquestioned face of the franchise, every offhand comment about who he'd want alongside him is likely to get outsized attention this offseason, especially with Jokić's own contract situation in Denver still unresolved heading into next summer.
Article Summary: Luka Doncic picked Nikola Jokić over LeBron James in a rapid-fire interview. Fans reacted with a mix of amusement and speculation. The pick drew attention because James recently informed the Lakers he will sign elsewhere in free agency.

Article Title: Georgina Rodríguez breaks silence after Cristiano Ronaldo's heartbreaking World Cup exit
Article Text:
Georgina Rodríguez and Cristiano Ronaldo. Image via: Daniele Venturelli/Daniele Venturelli/WireImage

Georgina Rodríguez pays tribute to Cristiano Ronaldo after Portugal's World Cup exit

Georgina Rodríguez pays tribute to Cristiano Ronaldo. Image via: georginagio/ Instagram

Cristiano Ronaldo's World Cup career ended in heartbreak

Georgina Rodríguez shared a Nike tribute to fiancé Cristiano Ronaldo on Instagram after Portugal's 1-0 World Cup elimination to Spain on July 6, a loss that ended Ronaldo's international career without a trophy. The 41-year-old finished with 11 goals and 27 World Cup appearances. Portugal coach Roberto Martínez also announced his resignation after the match. Rodríguez and Ronaldo, together since 2016 and engaged since August 2025, share two children, and neither has confirmed wedding plans or Ronaldo's future with the national team.Georgina Rodríguez responded to Cristiano Ronaldo's World Cup elimination by resharing a Nike tribute to her fiancé on her Instagram Stories Monday night. The post, captioned "The story continues @cristiano," carried the tagline "His Legend Lives On," and Rodríguez added a single red heart emoji in support. She had earlier posted that she and the couple's children were watching the match from home.The gesture came hours after Ronaldo's Portugal fell 1-0 to Spain in the round of 16, a result that ended the 41-year-old's World Cup career for good since he had already confirmed this would be his last appearance in the tournament.Rodríguez, the mother of two of Ronaldo's five children and his fiancée since August 2025, has become a fixture of his major career moments, and her public reaction mattered to fans processing the end of an era.Substitute Mikel Merino settled the match with a stoppage-time goal in the 91st minute, sending Spain through to the quarterfinals and Portugal home. Ronaldo played the full 90 minutes but managed only three shots, two on target, against Spain goalkeeper Unai Simón. After the final whistle, he wiped tears from his eyes while applauding fans at the stadium. He told reporters afterward, "I'm sad to leave the World Cup this way," adding that he was moving on with a clear conscience.Ronaldo leaves the tournament with 11 goals and 27 appearances across six World Cups, the second most behind Lionel Messi, but without the trophy that eluded him throughout his career. He won Euro 2016 and two Nations League titles with Portugal, and his deepest World Cup run remains a semifinal in 2006. Portugal coach Roberto Martínez also announced his own departure after the match, saying the team needed "a new voice" going forward.Rodríguez has spoken publicly about the ring only once, telling Elle Spain in December that it was "gorgeous" and joking it was "the least he could offer me after 10 years of waiting." The couple has not announced wedding plans, and with Martínez's exit reshaping Portugal's coaching staff and Ronaldo's own international future still unconfirmed, Rodríguez's next public statement is likely to carry even more weight than usual.
Article Summary: Georgina Rodríguez shared a Nike tribute to fiancé Cristiano Ronaldo on Instagram. The gesture came hours after Ronaldo's Portugal fell 1-0 to Spain in the round of 16. Portugal coach Roberto Martínez also announced his resignation after the match.

Article Title: Belgium trolls USA and celebrates with Donald Trump’s viral dance after FIFA World Cup 2026 win over hosts
Article Text:
Belgium troll USA and celebrate with Donald Trump’s viral dance after FIFA World Cup 2026 Win over hosts (via Getty)

Belgium send strong message after massive win over USA



Belgium sent a strong message both on and off the pitch after knocking the United States out of the FIFA World Cup with a convincing 4-1 victory in the Round of 16. Romelu Lukaku and his teammates sealed their place in the quarter-finals with an impressive display that left the hosts with no answers.The celebrations after the final whistle attracted almost as much attention as the result itself. Belgium players appeared to take aim at the controversy surrounding Folarin Balogun’s eligibility by performing a viral Donald Trump-inspired dance. The country’s official social media account also shared a post that many fans saw as another dig at the United States.Belgium looked in control from the opening whistle and made sure the pre-match controversy had no impact on the result. Belgium gave one of their best performances of the tournament to comfortably defeat the United States and book a quarter-final meeting with Spain.The biggest talking point after the game came during the celebrations. Romelu Lukaku scored Belgium’s fourth goal in stoppage time to complete the 4-1 victory. As his teammates gathered around him, several Belgium players performed the now-famous Donald Trump dance in front of the cameras.The celebration quickly spread across social media, with many fans linking it to the recent controversy involving the United States.Belgium’s official X account added more fuel to the discussion after the match. The team shared a photo of the players celebrating together and posted a short message telling people to “reverse this decision” in different words. The post was viewed as a sarcastic response to FIFA’s decision regarding Folarin Balogun.Balogun had been expected to miss the Round-of-16 clash after receiving a red card in the previous match. However, FIFA later suspended his automatic one-match ban, making him available for selection against Belgium. The decision created major debate before kickoff.Reports suggested that US President Donald Trump had spoken with FIFA president Gianni Infantino about the situation. Trump later said that while he discussed the issue, he did not pressure FIFA to change its decision. Belgium were unhappy with Balogun’s availability and submitted a challenge before the match, but FIFA rejected the appeal.Despite all the attention surrounding Balogun, the striker was unable to influence the game. Belgium’s defence kept him quiet throughout the contest, limiting his chances in front of goal.On the field, Belgium were clearly the better side. Charles De Ketelaere scored twice to put his team in control. Hans Vanaken then took advantage of a costly mistake by the American goalkeeper to extend the lead. Malik Tillman’s deflected free-kick briefly gave the home side hope, but Lukaku’s late goal ended any chance of a comeback.Belgium’s performance showed why they remain among the favourites to challenge for the World Cup title. With Spain waiting in the quarter-finals, Belgium will now look to carry their momentum into another huge test.
Article Summary: Belgium beat the United States 4-1 in the FIFA World Cup Round of 16. Charles De Ketelaere, Hans Vanaken and Romelu Lukaku scored for the Red Devils. Belgium will now face Spain in the quarter-finals.

Article Title: “So much disrespect”: Lamine Yamal’s playful airport antics leave fans divided ahead of Spain and Portugal showdown
Article Text:
Lamine Yamal’s playful airport antics leave fans divided ahead of Spain and Portugal showdown(Getty)

Fans split after Lamine Yamal’s airport joke on Spain teammates goes viral



Spain shift focus to Portugal as knockout challenge awaits

Lamine Yamal grabbed headlines for a reason that had nothing to do with football. Just hours before Spain's FIFA World Cup Round of 16 clash against Portugal, the 18-year-old shared a playful airport video that quickly went viral and divided fans online.The clip showed several Spain players waiting during a routine document check before boarding their flight. Yamal and Nico Williams had already made it onto the plane. Instead of sitting quietly, the Barcelona youngster pointed his phone down at his teammates and laughed as they waited to be cleared. Some fans loved the joke. Others felt it was disrespectful with a knockout match around the corner.The short video offered another behind-the-scenes glimpse into Spain's World Cup journey. Several players remained with airport security officials while their travel documents were checked. Meanwhile, Lamine Yamal and Williams had already boarded the aircraft.Seeing his teammates still waiting below, Yamal recorded the scene and laughed, making it look as though he and Williams had earned special treatment while the rest of the squad remained stuck at security. The lighthearted moment soon made the rounds on social media, where fans wasted little time sharing their opinions.One reaction quickly grabbed attention. A fan wrote, "So much disrespect in the World Cup." The comment reflected the view of those who believed Yamal's joke was poorly timed before Spain's biggest game of the tournament.Many others defended the teenager. One supporter commented, "Just a kid; let them enjoy."Another fan acknowledged his talent and wrote, "Kid in reality, lethal in the field.A different fan found the whole situation hilarious, posting, "Bro is really just filming his teammates getting put through it; he is so unserious." The remark summed up how many viewers saw the clip as harmless fun between teammates rather than anything disrespectful.Lamine Yamal's playful personality also became part of the discussion. One fan wrote, "Lamine Yamal like roasting his teammates." Another added, "The kid is just charming and funny."The contrasting reactions only added to the buzz around one of football's brightest young stars, proving that even a few seconds of behind-the-scenes footage can become a talking point during the World Cup.The airport video surfaced just before Spain's Round of 16 meeting with Portugal on July 6 in Dallas, one of the standout fixtures of the knockout stage. Spain have carefully managed Yamal's minutes after the hamstring injury that cut short his Barcelona season in April. He returned during the group stage and looked much sharper in Spain's Round of 32 victory over Austria. Ahead of the Portugal clash, Spain coach Luis de la Fuente said Yamal was in "perfect" condition for the knockout phase.Portugal, meanwhile, arrived with plenty of attention on Cristiano Ronaldo and whether the veteran captain would lead the attack in another high-pressure World Cup game. Spain entered the tournament unbeaten, while Portugal hoped to build on their dramatic victory over Croatia and book a place in the quarterfinals.Before either side stepped onto the field, though, it was Yamal's sense of humor that stole the spotlight. A simple airport video turned into one of the tournament's latest viral moments, reminding fans that the teenager continues to make headlines both on and off the pitch.
Article Summary: Lamine Yamal shared a playful airport video that quickly went viral and divided fans. The clip showed several Spain players waiting during a routine document check. Yamal and Nico Williams had already made it onto the plane. The lighthearted moment soon made the rounds on social media, where fans shared their opinions.

Article Title: ‘My prayers weren’t answered’: IShowSpeed’s Ronaldo heartbreak got messier after one Messi shout
Article Text:
IShowSpeed’s reaction to Portugal’s World Cup exit went viral after a Messi fan trolled him outside the stadium. (Images via Twitter/X)

IShowSpeed broke down after Cristiano Ronaldo’s final World Cup ended in Texas

“I thought they was gonna win it all, man. I thought they was gonna win it all, I swear to God, Wallahi,”

“I prayed. I prayed. I prayed, I prayed, and I prayed. I prayed… my prayers wasn’t answered,”

“I’m sad to leave the World Cup this way,”

“As I said yesterday, I gave it my all, and I leave with a clear conscience.

That’s the life of a footballer. You have to move forward.”

One Messi shout turned Portugal’s loss into a viral internet fight

Darren “IShowSpeed” Watkins Jr. went to Portugal vs. Spain hoping for one more Cristiano Ronaldo moment. Instead, he left AT&T Stadium in Arlington, Texas, in tears, with a Lionel Messi fan turning the knife on camera.Portugal lost 1-0 to Spain on Monday, July 6, in the FIFA World Cup Round of 16. Mikel Merino scored the winner in stoppage time, ending Portugal’s tournament and, by Ronaldo’s own admission, his World Cup career. For Speed, one of Ronaldo’s loudest online supporters, the night became content for all the wrong reasons.Speed’s reaction came almost immediately after the final whistle. The streamer, who has built a large part of his soccer identity around Ronaldo, sank into the moment on his livestream as Portugal’s run ended.He had spent the tournament backing Ronaldo and Portugal with the kind of all-in energy his audience expects. That made the exit hit harder. Speed did not try to dress it up as a normal loss. He looked stunned, then emotional.Speed said on the stream.He then put the loss in the simplest terms possible.he said.The pain made sense in context. Ronaldo entered the 2026 World Cup at 41, and this became his final run at the one trophy missing from his career. Reuters reported after the game that Ronaldo confirmed it was his last World Cup, though he did not immediately retire from Portugal duty.Ronaldo also addressed the defeat after the match.Ronaldo said.Speed’s stream turned that national-team ending into something more personal. He was watching Ronaldo’s World Cup door close in real time.The crying clip would have gone viral by itself. Then the exit from the stadium turned it into something sharper. Came the part the internet was always going to clip. As Speed left the stadium, a fan wearing a Messi jersey shouted the Argentina captain’s name in his direction. It was not a long speech. It did not need to be. For a streamer whose public soccer persona is built on Ronaldo fandom and Messi trolling, one name was enough.Speed snapped toward the fan and tried to move in his direction before bodyguards held him back and guided him away. The clip spread quickly on X and Instagram, partly because it looked like the Ronaldo-Messi debate had found Speed at the worst possible second.That is why the moment worked online. It was petty, perfectly timed and cruel in the way sports crowds can be cruel. It also landed because Speed has spent years making the rivalry part of his brand. When Portugal lost, the Messi side got its opening.Still, there is a line between taunting and escalation. The fan got the reaction he wanted. Speed nearly gave him a bigger one. Security stepping in kept the clip from becoming something worse.The result will not change. Spain are moving on. Portugal are out. Ronaldo’s World Cup story is over. For Speed, the night ended with the two words he probably wanted to hear least: Messi’s name.
Article Summary: Portugal lost 1-0 to Spain in the FIFA World Cup Round of 16. Darren “IShowSpeed” Watkins Jr. was one of Ronaldo’s loudest online supporters. As Speed left the stadium, a fan wearing a Messi jersey shouted the Argentina captain's name in his direction. The fan got the reaction he wanted.

Article Title: 'Paraguay's players were absolute disgrace': South American side slammed after FIFA World Cup exit to France
Article Text:
Paraguay's Matias Galarza (23) talks with France's Kylian Mbappe (10) (AP Photo/Martin Meissner)

France booked their place in the World Cup quarter-finals with a hard-fought 1-0 win over Paraguay, but much of the attention after the match focused on Paraguay's behaviour rather than the result.After impressing fans by eliminating Germany on penalties in the previous round, Paraguay faced criticism for their aggressive tactics in a heated and ill-tempered contest in Philadelphia.Speaking to BBC One, Former England goalkeeper Joe Hart did not hold back, saying: "Paraguay's players were an absolute disgrace. If they were on my team, I'd be dragging them off the pitch."Several incidents involving Paraguay players went unpunished, including challenges on Adrien Rabiot and Kylian Mbappe , while France players received multiple yellow cards. Former England defender Micah Richards said: "It was embarrassing to see. Paraguay are better than that. Defensively they were so good and they didn't need to get into these antics."The game's decisive moment came 20 minutes from time when Desire Doue was brought down in the box. After reviewing the incident on the monitor, referee Ilgiz Tantashev awarded France a penalty.Paraguay players protested heavily and attempted to delay the spot-kick, with Gustavo Velazquez even trying to damage the penalty spot before Mbappe converted to score his seventh goal of the tournament, drawing level with Lionel Messi in the Golden Boot race.France coach Didier Deschamps later revealed: "There were some insults form the other bench we could have done without."Tensions continued after the final whistle as players clashed again. Former Scotland winger Pat Nevin summed up Paraguay's approach by saying: "Paraguay tried to wind up the France players, little shoves and nudges but not enough to get a yellow card. Every single dark art you can use."France will now face Morocco in the quarter-finals.
Article Summary: France beat Paraguay 1-0 to reach the World Cup quarter-finals. But much of the attention after the match focused on Paraguay's behaviour. Several incidents involving Paraguay players went unpunished. France will now face Morocco in the quarter-final.

Article Title: 'How has Vaibhav Sooryavanshi not played?' : Ex-England captain baffled by India's decision
Article Text:
India's Vaibhav Sooryavanshi (ANI Photo)

Poll Vote & Share your view Who should be dropped to accommodate Sooryavanshi in the starting XI? Abhishek Sharma Sanju Samson 3k+ users shared opinion today 5k+ users already voted today 3k+ users shared opinion today Share Opinion

The excitement around Vaibhav Sooryavanshi has gone far beyond India, with the 15-year-old attracting attention from cricket fans across the world. Such has been the hype surrounding the teenage sensation that tickets for India's T20Is in Ireland were snapped up by supporters hoping to watch him in action. However, Sooryavanshi remained on the sidelines throughout the two-match series.The story did not change after India travelled to Durham for the T20Is against England. Despite the growing anticipation around his international debut, the youngster once again had to settle for a place on the bench. His continued omission has left several former cricketers puzzled, including Ravi Shastri and Sunil Gavaskar . Now, former England captains Michael Vaughan and Alastair Cook have also questioned India's decision.Speaking on the Stick to Cricket podcast, Vaughan admitted he could not understand why Sooryavanshi had not been given an opportunity, even suggesting he would like to put the question directly to the BCCI and chairman of selectors Ajit Agarkar.“We must say to the BCCI and the selector, Ajit, how has he not played against Ireland? Our mate, the Kid,” said Vaughan on the Stick to Cricket podcast, to which Cook replied: “I’m there tomorrow, and I’m driving up as a fan to make sure that the 15-year-old kid plays.”While Vaughan and Cook's comments reflected the growing curiosity over Sooryavanshi's absence, the chances of the Indian team management changing course appear slim. The selectors and coaching staff have consistently maintained that the teenager's opportunity will come at the appropriate time.At present, Sooryavanshi's path into the playing XI is blocked by Abhishek Sharma and Sanju Samson , both of whom remain firmly established in India's T20I side. Having played key roles in India's third T20 World Cup triumph four months ago, neither batter is expected to lose his place anytime soon.Even so, Sooryavanshi's omission has continued to spark debate considering the extraordinary rise he has enjoyed over the past five months. The left-hander first captained India Under-19 to the World Cup title, smashing a breathtaking 175 in the final. He then carried that momentum into the IPL, piling up 776 runs to become the youngest-ever Orange Cap winner.Before joining the senior squad for the Ireland tour, Sooryavanshi also impressed for India A in Sri Lanka. His match-winning 94 in the final included the fastest half-century in List A cricket and played a decisive role in India's comprehensive victory, further strengthening the case of those eager to see the teenage prodigy make his international debut.
Article Summary: India's Vaibhav Sooryavanshi is yet to make his international debut. The 15-year-old was left on the bench for India's T20Is against England. Michael Vaughan and Alastair Cook have questioned the selectors' decision. Abhishek Sharma and Sanju Samson remain firmly established in the side.

Article Title: 'Tougher days will come': Ex-India cricketer's reality check for Vaibhav Sooryavanshi
Article Text:
Vaibhav Sooryavanshi (Image: IANS)

India face England in Manchester

Former India wicketkeeper-batter Dinesh Karthik has lavished praise on teenage sensation Vaibhav Sooryavanshi, calling him a rare talent capable of rewriting T20 records, while also urging the youngster to stay grounded amid the growing spotlight.The 15-year-old batting prodigy has emerged as one of India's brightest prospects after his remarkable rise in domestic and IPL. He is likely to make his senior India debut during the ongoing T20I series against England, expectations around Sooryavanshi continue to soar.Speaking about the youngster's potential in Sky Sports, Karthik highlighted Sooryavanshi's fearless mindset and ability to score freely against any opposition."I think it's just his ability to score all around the ground and the tempo at which he bats. The fact that he doesn't get fazed by whoever his opponent is stands out. That fearlessness is a special quality," Karthik said.While acknowledging the teenager's immense talent, Karthik reminded everyone that every young cricketer goes through challenging phases, both on and off the field."Like everybody in life, I'm sure he will face challenges with his batting and things that happen away from cricket.He needs to learn how to deal with those situations. Right now, everything looks rosy, but tougher days will come," he added.Asked how far Sooryavanshi could go in the game, Karthik left little room for doubt."The sky is the limit. I genuinely feel he can become an extraordinary T20 batter, and I don't see why he can't break many records in the format," he said."My one piece of advice would be to learn to deal with the noise off the field in a healthy manner because that's going to be his biggest challenge in today's world," Karthik concluded.India and England will look to break the deadlock when they meet in the second T20I at Old Trafford, Manchester, after the series opener in Durham was washed out by rain. Despite the no result, India would have taken confidence from posting a competitive 189/7, powered by half-centuries from Abhishek Sharma and captain Shreyas Iyer.
Article Summary: Former India wicketkeeper-batter Dinesh Karthik has lavished praise on teenage sensation Vaibhav Sooryavanshi. The 15-year-old batting prodigy has emerged as one of India's brightest prospects. He is likely to make his senior India debut during the ongoing T20I series.
